text.title: web-scraping-pagination Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for web-scraping-pagination as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Checking only "button exists" without checking disabled state or viewport visibility for next/load-more termination.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Many sites keep the button in the DOM but set it to disabled or visually hidden on the last page; the loop never terminates.

failure-mode.description: In infinite scroll, re-collecting all items each iteration without slicing by previous count.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Produces duplicate rows every iteration because already-collected items are still in the DOM.

failure-mode.description: Launching a new browser per URL in a large batch instead of reusing a pool.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Browser launch adds 1–3 seconds of overhead and creates a new Chromium process for each URL; 100 URLs = 100 Chromium processes running simultaneously.
